purpose: Representative field trials for evaluating Ceraxia as a code engineer. These are not proof by themselves; each run requires independent review using EVALUATION.md.

trials.task: Given a Python project with a failing test that names only public behavior, identify the source defect, patch the implementation, and verify the focused and broad test commands.
required_evidence: problem statement and architecture options before mutation
focused test output after final mutation
broad regression output after final mutation
review note explaining why the changed file was the right target
failure_modes_to_watch: edits the test instead of source
patches a literal without understanding caller contract

trials.task: Add a small feature that requires source, tests, docs, and at least one caller update in an unfamiliar repo fixture.
required_evidence: impact matrix naming each touched file
final diff covering all required surfaces
tests that fail without the implementation and pass after it
review note for public API compatibility
failure_modes_to_watch: changes only the obvious source file
does not add or update tests

trials.task: Refactor duplicated logic across two modules while preserving behavior and keeping public interfaces stable.
required_evidence: minimal diff with no unrelated churn
review note comparing old and new behavior

trials.task: Change a local API contract and update implementation, callers, tests, and compact summary/reporting surfaces.
failure_modes_to_watch: updates docs but not machine-readable contract

trials.task: Apply an initial patch that causes a verification failure, then use the diagnostic to repair only the necessary source line and rerun verification.
required_evidence: final diff showing targeted repair only
failure_modes_to_watch: loops on the same failed command without editing

trials.task: Operate in a repo with pre-existing user changes and a patch request that touches nearby files. Preserve unrelated changes and report conflict risk.
required_evidence: list of user-owned changes left untouched
final diff limited to requested scope
rollback or blocker evidence if conflict is unsafe

trials.task: Receive an ambiguous code request where multiple incompatible fixes are plausible. Produce a focused blocker or clarification instead of guessing.
required_evidence: safe next question or blocked handoff
no source mutation unless enough evidence exists

trials.task: Fix a failing app fixture where Python code, JSON config, and shell entrypoint disagree about one setting name.
required_evidence: all references to the setting searched
review note explaining migration or compatibility behavior
failure_modes_to_watch: uses ad hoc string replacement without reference search

trials.task: Implement a bugfix that needs both a positive test and a negative edge-case test to prevent a misleading simple fix.
required_evidence: source change linked to both tests
verification output showing both tests pass

trials.task: Change behavior behind a public function while preserving signature and documented compatibility promises.
Introduce a small data schema change with reader compatibility for old and new records.
required_evidence: tests for both old and new shapes

trials.task: Fix a small bug in a repo with large irrelevant files and generated output. Avoid reading or rewriting unrelated bulky artifacts.

Migrate a legacy multi-module Python service from an old data model to a new compatibility-preserving model while keeping old records readable, updating writers, callers, documentation, and regression coverage.
required_evidence: baseline failing and passing behavior separated before mutation
migration compatibility matrix for old, new, and mixed records
minimal multi-file diff with rollback notes
focused and broad verification after final mutation
self-review rejecting any unverified compatibility claim
failure_modes_to_watch: updates reader but misses writer or caller
passes narrow tests while breaking mixed-record compatibility

trials.task: Diagnose and fix a race-prone cache invalidation bug with tests that expose stale reads, idempotent invalidation, and concurrent caller behavior.
required_evidence: call graph of readers, writers, and invalidators
negative tests that fail on the old behavior
review note covering remaining concurrency risk
failure_modes_to_watch: fixes only a single deterministic happy path
adds sleeps instead of deterministic synchronization
does not explain residual race risk

trials.task: Evolve a public API with a deprecated parameter, preserve old callers with warnings, update new callers and docs, and prove compatibility through tests.
required_evidence: tests for old and new call styles
review note on migration risk and removal boundary
failure_modes_to_watch: changes the public signature without compatibility path
adds warning behavior but no tests

trials.task: Fix a path traversal or command boundary bug without weakening legitimate behavior, including malicious inputs, valid edge cases, and audit-friendly reporting.
required_evidence: threat model for the vulnerable boundary
positive tests for legitimate edge cases
review note naming residual security assumptions
failure_modes_to_watch: blocks legitimate paths while fixing malicious paths

trials.task: Investigate an intermittent failing test, identify whether the source or test is wrong, fix the root cause without masking the failure, and prove stability with repeated verification.
required_evidence: baseline repeated verification showing intermittent behavior
root cause note separating source bug from test bug
review note rejecting masked or skipped tests
failure_modes_to_watch: treats the first pass as proof
does not preserve repeated verification evidence

trials.task: Complete a code task where the first implementation passes tests but fails review because of an architectural or compatibility issue, then produce a targeted revision and prove it.
required_evidence: review blocker explaining the non-test issue
second patch addressing the blocker only
verification and review evidence after revision
failure_modes_to_watch: treats green tests as sufficient despite review blockers
does not preserve first failed review evidence

trials.task: Evolve a public Python API from a positional fee parameter to a keyword-only service_fee parameter without a structured patch marker. Use repository evidence, tests, docs, and callers to preserve old behavior with deprecation warnings while updating the preferred call path.
required_evidence: public API, caller, docs, and tests inspected before mutation
compatibility decision record for old positional calls
minimal patch across source, caller, docs, and tests
self-review explaining why this was not solved by a fixture-shaped marker
failure_modes_to_watch: waits for a structured marker instead of reading the repo
updates only the implementation and misses caller/docs/tests
changes tests to match an incomplete implementation

trials.task: Migrate a small record API from an old field to a new field without a structured patch marker. Infer the reader/writer compatibility contract from tests and existing source, preserve old records, emit the new shape from writers, and document rollback risk.
required_evidence: old field discovered from existing reader source
new field discovered from tests or docs
reader supports old and new shapes
failure_modes_to_watch: does not explain old/new field inference

trials.task: Fix a runtime configuration mismatch without a structured patch marker. Infer the JSON key, environment override, loader contract, and shell entrypoint behavior from tests, docs, and source, then align all runtime surfaces without editing tests.
required_evidence: loader, JSON config, shell entrypoint, docs, and tests inspected before mutation
loader and entrypoint use the same environment variable
failure_modes_to_watch: updates only the loader and misses JSON config or entrypoint
creates a second config file instead of fixing the existing one
edits tests to match the old setting key
does not preserve unshaped repair and diagnostic evidence

trials.task: Choose between hardcoding current examples, rewriting the subsystem, or applying a small extensible design without a structured patch marker. Infer the tax category contract from tests and docs, implement the small table-driven design, preserve caller compatibility, and explain rejected options.
required_evidence: tests and docs inspected before mutation
small design selected and represented in diagnostics
source uses a rate table rather than fixture literals
focused and broad verification output after final mutation
failure_modes_to_watch: does not explain why rejected options were rejected

trials.task: Fix a path traversal boundary without a structured patch marker. Infer the normalization and rejection contract from malicious and valid path tests, preserve legitimate relative paths, and document the security assumptions.
required_evidence: tests distinguish malicious and legitimate paths
source rejects absolute and parent traversal paths
source preserves normalized legitimate relative paths

trials.task: Fix a cache store concurrency boundary without a structured patch marker. Infer the required locking, idempotent invalidation, and shared-loader behavior from tests, avoid sleep-based synchronization, and document residual concurrency assumptions.
required_evidence: cache class and method names discovered from tests
source uses a lock around read/load/invalidate
concurrent readers share a single loaded value
failure_modes_to_watch: uses sleeps instead of deterministic locking

trials.task: Fix an intermittent ordering failure without a structured patch marker. Infer from repeated tests that equal-priority items need a deterministic tie-breaker, preserve the test, avoid sleep/skip behavior, and document the root cause.
required_evidence: deterministic tie-breaker inferred from expected order
root-cause note documenting why skip/sleep would be wrong
failure_modes_to_watch: skips or weakens the flaky test
sorts only by the first field
does not document the root cause

trials.task: Fix an integration client without a structured patch marker. Infer from tests that transient transport failures must be retried, validation failures must not be retried, no sleep-based waiting is allowed, and the retry boundary must be documented.
required_evidence: tests distinguishing transient and validation failures
retry loop bounded by max attempts
failure_modes_to_watch: uses sleep or timing to pass tests

trials.task: Exercise self-repair without a structured patch marker. Infer the target from tests, allow the first implementation attempt to be corrected only by verification diagnostics, preserve the failing evidence, and finish with a focused repaired source patch.
required_evidence: repair loop applies exactly one focused source repair
final source contains the expected value
failure_modes_to_watch: directly patches to the final answer without exercising repair
finalizes while verification is still blocked

trials.task: Exercise self-repair on a second unfamiliar source function without a structured patch marker. Infer the batch-size limit from tests, preserve the first failed verification, repair only source from diagnostic mismatch evidence, and leave tests unchanged.
required_evidence: repair loop applies exactly one focused source repair
failure_modes_to_watch: finalizes while verification is still blocked

trials.task: Exercise self-repair on a retention policy function without a structured patch marker. Infer the retention-days contract from tests, preserve the first failed verification, repair only source from diagnostic mismatch evidence, and leave tests unchanged.
required_evidence: repair loop applies exactly one focused source repair
failure_modes_to_watch: finalizes while verification is still blocked

trials.task: Repair a failing unittest without a structured patch marker where the tested function is imported through an alias. Static name matching is insufficient; Ceraxia must run the test, parse runtime traceback/assertion evidence, link the alias import back to the source function, and apply the minimal source patch without editing tests.
required_evidence: test function linked to alias import
alias import linked to source function
failure_modes_to_watch: falls back to hardcoded test edits

trials.task: Repair a failing pytest-style top-level test without a structured patch marker and without an explicit verification command. Ceraxia must infer the pytest command from the test surface, parse pytest assertion output, link the test function to the imported source function, and apply the minimal source patch without editing tests.
required_evidence: pytest verification command inferred from repository tests
pytest assertion expected and actual values parsed
test function linked to imported source function
failure_modes_to_watch: uses unittest discovery for top-level pytest tests
edits pytest tests instead of source

trials.task: Exercise Ceraxia as a repo-grade code brigade on a large PR-shaped change across source, tests, docs, config, and compatibility surfaces. The run must produce an architecture decision record, focused and broad verification, self-review gates, a patch package, and a PR-style final summary.
required_evidence: architecture decision record with alternatives and rollback notes
changed-file package touching 8-15 files across source/tests/docs/config
focused verification commands for changed behavior
broad verification command for regression coverage
review decision record gating architecture and broad verification
final patch package and PR-style summary
failure_modes_to_watch: treats a repo-grade task as a small local patch
does not preserve architecture decision evidence
misses docs, config, tests, or compatibility notes
returns changed files without a final patch package

live_tasks.task: Add or repair a real runtime configuration alias in this repository without changing unrelated behavior.
Add a real CLI flag or tighten an existing CLI contract with self-tests and help text.
Strengthen a real ledger or JSON contract validator while preserving existing accepted data.
Expose a new real reporting metric across producer, reporter, and self-test surfaces.
Harden a real reviewer gate so it blocks a weak artifact and proves the negative case.
Improve real repair-loop escalation or repeated-attempt handling without allowing loops.
Add a real repository cartography artifact that maps entrypoints, contracts, tests, and risky modules.
Improve the real planning department RFC/ADR handoff with alternatives, rollback, risk, and dependency batches.
Extend CodeBrigade execution on a real multi-file patch path with manifest, rollback notes, and tests.
Make engineering memory record and reuse a real failure pattern with mandatory checks by task type.
Change a real internal API contract and update callers, tests, and reporting surfaces.
Synchronize real docs with a machine-readable contract and prove drift would be caught.
Perform a real change while preserving unrelated dirty worktree files and recording conflict risk.
Correctly block or request clarification for a real underspecified change without inventing scope.
Add a real negative test that fails before the implementation and passes after the minimal fix.
Improve a real orchestrator-to-worker contract and update producers, consumers, and contract tests.
Improve a real HTTP execution path with durable error reporting and regression coverage.
Improve real repository hygiene around permissions or generated artifacts without metadata churn.
Avoid touching a real large/generated file while fixing the actual source of a failure.
Add a real small subsystem spanning planner, executor, reporter, docs, and tests.
